lyrics:
Watching, when the world still dreamed itself there
Speaking not what the Gods were wont to hear
Cursed as the inbetween of change and choice
The hero's halting step or plaintive fall
The face of the gate-I remember all
If the way reversed, return my dark voice

I am Umbra Ala
Keeper of the gate
Speaker of truth
Temptress of fate

He came, not crowned, but cracked at the seam
Carrying nothing but his unspoken dream
His hands were shaking, but he met my stare
"You guard the gate-do you guard your pain?"
And something within me stirred at the name
That none had asked, nor thought to dare

I am Umbra Ala
Keeper of the gate
Speaker of truth
Temptress of fate

I turned from stone, and the gate turned too
The silence broke like a sky split through
My feathers flared where the light once died
No longer bound by vow or fate
I crossed the threshold I helped create
And flew, not fallen-but justified

I am Umbra Ala
Keeper of the gate
Speaker of truth
Temptress of fate

I am Umbra Ala
Speaker of truth
Temptress of fate
Many ways… swings the gate
persona:
## 4Castle

The Future of Rock, The Power of Harmony

**4Castle **is a genre-defying, five-piece supergroup under Maricopa Records, built on a foundation of powerful vocal harmonies, intricate musicianship, and fearless innovation. Rooted in rock, but unbound by limitations, their music fuses elements of progressive rock, funk, Latin rhythms, blues, and folk, delivering a sound that is both technically complex and emotionally resonant.

### **A Band Like No Other**

Each member of **4Castle **brings a distinct voice-both musically and vocally-to the mix:
🎸 **Raven Cortez**(Lead Guitar, Vocals) – A fiery virtuoso with Latin and blues influences, weaving face-melting solos into every performance.
🎸 **Skylar “Sky” Monroe**(Rhythm Guitar, Vocals) – The poetic soul of the band, crafting melodic foundations and lyrically rich storytelling.
🎹 **Jonah Vega**(Keyboards, Vocals) – The sonic architect, layering psychedelic synths, Hammond organ swells, and cinematic orchestrations.
🎸 **Malcolm “Mack” Bishop**(Bass, Vocals) – The groove master, blending funk, jazz, and progressive rock into fluid, deep-pocket basslines.
🥁 **Eli “Fox” Foster **(Drums, Vocals) – The wild heartbeat, pushing the band forward with thunderous fills, polyrhythmic grooves, and explosive energy.

### **What Defines 4Castle?**

- **Multi-Layered Vocal Harmonies**– All five members **sing**, creating **rich, choral-like textures**that elevate every song.

- **Complex Arrangements & Bold Songwriting**– Each track is an **immersive journey**, balancing **technical mastery with raw emotion**.

- **Rhythmic Power & Innovation**– From **tight funk grooves to shifting time signatures**, their music **moves you-literally and figuratively**.

- **Lyrically Challenging Yet Accessible**– Their songs explore **deep themes of life, change, and resilience**, wrapped in **memorable hooks and melodies**.

### **4Castle’s Sound**

Imagine putting Emerson, Lake & Palmer, Heart, Led Zeppelin, Sly Stone, and Carlos Santana in a blender. The result? **4Castle**. Their music pays homage to the greats while pushing rock’s boundaries into the future.

### **Join the Journey**

With Maricopa Records, **4Castle **is set to redefine what a modern rock band can be. Expect genre-bending albums, electrifying performances, and music that resonates across generations.

🚀 Follow **4Castle **as they build their legacy-one song, one harmony, and one unforgettable moment at a time.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Cmxf7xuTHzf7UahMK2xJh)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/4castle/1801052673)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/5-rooks-in-a-dark-setting-1.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track establishes a narrative, almost mythic atmosphere with a clean, driving electric guitar and clear, storytelling vocals, creating a sense of anticipation from the start. The energy builds from a measured, folk-rock verse into a powerful, anthemic chorus with soaring vocals and thick, distorted guitars, giving the song a dramatic, climactic feel. The production is clean and dynamic, with a well-balanced mix that allows the powerful lead vocal to sit clearly above the crunchy guitars and solid rhythm section, creating a sound that feels both spacious and dense in its louder moments. A defining quality is the explosive shift into the chorus at 0:57, where the full band erupts, transforming the track's mood from contemplative to a powerful, declarative statement that solidifies its epic rock identity.

review.review_text: "Umbra Ala" arrives in 3/4 time at a pace that should feel relentless but instead breathes like a war ballad, and the concept of a mythic gatekeeper who earns her own freedom gives the lyrics real weight: "I turned from stone, and the gate turned too" is the kind of line that lands differently the second time through. The folk-rock verse builds carefully before the full band erupts just past the midpoint, distorted guitars and stacked vocals turning the chorus into something closer to early Heart than anything on modern rock radio. At 3:42 in E minor, it doesn't overstay its welcome, and the final variation, "Many ways swings the gate," closes the mythology with just enough ambiguity to send you back to the beginning. A genuine highlight.
